title: Going Eva Crazy
wikitext:
[]: Going Eva Crazy             Quote Post by Gold_guardian » Wed May 18, 2011 2:31 am I searched to try and make sure this isn't a redundant thread. I didn't see anything else so I hope I'm right and that it's in the right spot too. Lately I've been a bit obsessed with evangelion. I'd seen some of it in the past before back in the day. Out of curiosity I rented the vhs that was available at the local rogers video when it opened and saw the first 9 or so episodes and didn't really get why people where calling it "THE GREATEST ANIME OF ALL TIME" I've seen episodes here and there since then at Anime conventions, but never the entire series. So when the Rebuild movies came along I decided to jump in and watched a fansub version prior to the legal english release of 2.22 You can (not) advance. Now I almost never buy dvds or Blu-ray any more for various reasons. (Low budget, Not having a full time job ect) but since Rebuilt is only supposed to be 4 movies and only the first two are out now I decided it was an okay to pick it up since I wouldn't be spending that much. On the second view of the movies back to back this time I liked them even more, especially since they seem like they could be a sequel of sorts to the series and not just a retelling. Now I knew more or less what happens in the series but curious to see it first hand and flush with money from a tax return I decided to treat myself and pick up the original series. At first I thought I'd save it so I'd have something to get at Anime North this year if I can go at the end of the month. But doing research on the price of the Platinum edition I found out that their was no way to get it for cheaper then $47 (including shipping) online thanks to it being on sale at Rightstuff. I'd have been lucky to pay $70 at the Convention if not more. But it didn't stop their. Somehow this has blown into a full on obsession. Wanting to read the manga I checked to see if their was a scanaltion online to read the first couple chapters to see if I would like it enough to consider buying it to add to my massive manga library. So I ending up spending $64 to get the first 9 volumes of The Shinji Ikari Raising Project manga published by darkhorse. Again Bought it online since at the convention it would have cost me a minimum of $80 since books NEVER go below Us cover price at a con unless you are buying a LOT more then I'm willing to spend right now. Now I'd trying to acquire the out of print Angelic days manga by ADV and the only reason I haven't gotten End of evangelion is becuse the dvd is out of print and crazy expensive, plus I've heard the quality is kind of low so I'm banking on Funimation doing a re-release once they can get the license eventually so I can get a legal version. The dvd set came in on friday and I've been watching one disc a night since. At this rate I'm going to finish the series on Thursday and then follow up with an EoE fansub on Saturday. No way am I watching something THAT mind warping on a work night lol. Oh did I mention I'm looking at getting the manga adaption of the main story as well and not just the spin offs. The Viz edition has several books out of print making it difficult to order online, but I've heard they are easy to find at conventions and such... So I started out with the plan to get only rebuild and ended up with an extra $100 down the hole and still climbing... How much further will my obsessive compulsive nature take me? The show and movies aren't nearly "best of all time" but I'm really enjoying them none the less.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Wed May 18, 2011 2:40 am get a better job

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Wed May 18, 2011 3:03 am Then there is Evangelion: Campus Apocalypse. Good luck collecting!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed May 18, 2011 4:16 am There is kind of a thread that pertains to this subject. It's something I could never relate to, because once I bought the Platinum Edition of the series and EoE I was pretty much content. Since then I've only bought the two Rebuilds on blu-ray and haven't had the slightest inkling to get any more merchandise except for soundtracks.

[]: Re: Going Eva Crazy             Quote Post by symbv » Wed May 18, 2011 4:41 am Gold_guardian wrote: So I started out with the plan to get only rebuild and ended up with an extra $100 down the hole and still climbing... How much further will my obsessive compulsive nature take me? The show and movies aren't nearly "best of all time" but I'm really enjoying them none the less.

[]: Re: Going Eva Crazy             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed May 18, 2011 7:19 am Gold_guardian wrote: The Viz edition has several books out of print making it difficult to order online, but I've heard they are easy to find at conventions and such...

[]: Re: Going Eva Crazy             Quote Post by Gold_guardian » Wed May 18, 2011 11:00 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Not true. I've never seen a copy of volume 9 at a convention. And I usually look to see if anyone has it. I figure I can either resell it to community member, or eBay it for profit. Never happens.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed May 18, 2011 9:34 pm I always feel a little ashamed to call myself a Eva fan when the only thing I've bought are the DVDs. Maybe there's a good t-shirt somewhere... And don't worry Gold Guardian, you're not truly obsessed with Eva until you get an Asuka tattoo on your arm. That's a joke, don't actually get an Asuka tattoo.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu May 19, 2011 6:38 am I'm getting more and more excited over the original NGE series myself. I'm not sure why that phase is creeping onto me again, other than the fact that, yes, it is awesome and well executed. Perhaps even the best of the Kaiju genre. EDIT: Oh! I know why! I first saw Neon Genesis Evangelion on YouTube back in May-June of 2009 while I was waiting for footage from a TV pilot to render in Final Cut Pro. This would be my second year anniversary of first seeing that show. And I didn't realize that last year was the first year anniversary either. Yeah. After we wrap production it is SO time to bust out that DVD collection and marathon that sucker all the way through The End of Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu May 19, 2011 8:20 am you can be something/someone's fan even if you don't get any merchandise/materials related to them. sometimes even more and as hardcore as the fans who buys all the shit. it's not substantial anyway, it's deep down in your heart <3 (plus i'm very dissapointed that nobody has yet to point this out yet, i am very, very dissapointed. )

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu May 19, 2011 9:31 am Trajan wrote: I always feel a little ashamed to call myself a Eva fan when the only thing I've bought are the DVDs. Maybe there's a good t-shirt somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by Gold_guardian » Thu May 19, 2011 6:47 pm Trajan wrote: I always feel a little ashamed to call myself a Eva fan when the only thing I've bought are the DVDs. Maybe there's a good t-shirt somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu May 19, 2011 10:37 pm Trajan wrote: I always feel a little ashamed to call myself a Eva fan when the only thing I've bought are the DVDs.
